content.problemStatement:
Chefland is holding a virtual marathon for the categories 101010 km, 212121 km and 424242 km having prizes A,B,CA, B, CA,B,C (A<B<CA \lt B \lt CA<B<C) respectively to promote physical fitness. In order to claim the prize in a particular category the person must cover the total distance for that category within DDD days. Also a single person **cannot** apply in multiple categories.

Given the maximum distance ddd km that Chef can cover in a single day, find the maximum prize that Chef can win at the end of DDD days. If Chef can't win any prize, print 000.


Input Format

The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
Each test case contains a single line of input, five integers D,d,A,B,CD, d, A, B, CD,d,A,B,C.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the answer to the problem.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains five integers D d A B C separated by spaces. D – number of days, d – maximum distance Chef can run in a single day, A, B, C – prize values for the 10 km, 21 km and 42 km categories respectively (A < B < C).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single integer – the maximum prize Chef can win. Print 0 if Chef cannot complete any category.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: total distance = 1*1 = 1 < 10 → no prize → 0.\nTest case 2: total = 10*1 = 10 ≥ 10 but < 21 → prize A = 1.\nTest case 3: total = 10*3 = 30 ≥ 21 but < 42 → prize B = 2.

solution.approach: For each test case compute the total distance Chef can cover: total = D * d. Compare total with the three fixed category distances (10, 21, 42) starting from the largest. The first category that fits determines the prize: if total ≥ 42 → prize C, else if total ≥ 21 → prize B, else if total ≥ 10 → prize A, else prize 0. This greedy check is optimal because the categories are independent and prizes increase with distance.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T; if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1) return 0; while(T--){ long long D,d,A,B,C; scanf("%lld %lld %lld %lld %lld",&D,&d,&A,&B,&C); long long total=D*d; long long ans=0; if(total>=42) ans=C; else if(total>=21) ans=B; else if(total>=10) ans=A; printf("%lld\n",ans);} return 0;}
